title: Public Wi-Fi Safety
drDigitalIntro: Public Wi-Fi at cafés and airports is convenient but risky — hackers can intercept your data. Never do sensitive things on public Wi-Fi: don't log into banking, don't enter credit cards, don't check email passwords. If you must, use a VPN (Virtual Private Network) that encrypts your connection. When you're home or on trusted networks, it's safe. Know the difference between safe and risky places to browse.
drDigitalHint: VPN encrypts your data so hackers can't see it.
